extractions.problem: Existing in silico CPP prediction methods produce a large number of false-positive results due to insufficient and non-diverse negative training data.
extractions.method: Ensemble deep learning (5 LSTM+attention models) trained on 9-mer sliding window fragments, using 11M human reference protein-derived peptides as negative set to reduce false positives.
extractions.finding: AiCPP outperformed MLCPP, CellPPD, and CPPred with higher specificity, and identified novel CPPs from amyloid precursor protein that were experimentally validated and optimized.
extractions.dataset: 2,798 unique CPPs (5-38 aa) from CellPPD/MLCPP/CPPsite 2.0; 11,046,343 negative 9-mers from 113,620 human reference proteins; test set of 150 CPP + 150 non-CPP
extractions.metric: AUC, MCC, ACC, SEN, SPE, PRE
extractions.result: AUC=0.927, MCC=0.722, ACC=0.860, SPE=0.893 (vs MLCPP AUC=0.882, CellPPD AUC=0.724, CPPred AUC=0.845)

extractions.problem: Computational tissue models either describe few cells in high detail or larger ensembles coarsely; bridging these two scales for large-scale 3D tissue simulation is computationally prohibitive.
extractions.method: Highly parallel cellular Potts model (CPM) on the NAStJA HPC framework with domain decomposition, halo exchange, and agent-based cellular events driving multi-scale simulation.
extractions.finding: The parallelized CPM introduces no directional bias from domain subdivision and scales approximately linearly beyond 10,000 cores, enabling 1000^3 voxel cancerous tissue simulation at sub-cellular resolution.
extractions.dataset: Synthetic 3D tissue simulations (2-cell verification, cancerous tissue 1000^3 voxels)
extractions.metric: Volume/surface ratio deviation, cell center displacement, parallel scaling efficiency
extractions.result: ~3% temporal variation in surface/volume, 0.4 voxel center drift (vs 10 voxel cell extent), linear scaling beyond 10,000 cores

extractions.problem: The interplay between genetic risk variants and epigenetic regulatory elements in primary Sjogren's syndrome (SS) is poorly understood, particularly which cell types are most affected.
extractions.method: In silico analysis of 72 SS-associated SNPs using VEP, RegulomeDB, and HaploReg v2 for regulatory motif enrichment, linkage disequilibrium screening, and histone marker analysis across cell types (B cells, monocytes, epithelial cells).
extractions.finding: 94.4% of SS risk variants showed regulatory function enrichment; transcriptionally active motifs were most enriched in B cells (> monocytes > T cells); active histone markers at promoters and enhancers predominated in B cells.
extractions.dataset: 72 SNPs from 43 SS gene risk factors; ENCODE/Roadmap Epigenome ChIP data for GM12878 (B cells), CD14+ (monocytes), A549 (epithelial)
extractions.metric: Pearson's Chi-squared test (P<0.05), regulatory motif enrichment percentage, LD r²≥0.8
extractions.result: 94.4% regulatory enrichment; 645 LD variants identified; B cell-specific active histone markers at promoters and enhancers

extractions.problem: Very few Leishmania major CD8+ T cell epitopes have been mapped for human HLA types, hindering polytope vaccine development against leishmaniasis.
extractions.method: In silico epitope prediction using 7 algorithms (SYFPEITHI, BIMAS, EpiJen, Rankpep, nHLApred, NetCTL, Multipred) for HLA-A*0201-restricted 9-mer peptides from 6 L. major antigens, validated by in vitro PBMC stimulation with intracellular cytokine assay, ELISpot, and ELISA.
extractions.finding: LmsTI-1 (pool II) and LPG-3 (pool IV) peptides elicited specific CD8+/IFN-γ+ T cell responses in HLA-A2+ recovered individuals, confirmed by ELISpot and ELISA.
extractions.dataset: 6 L. major antigens (CPB, CPC, LmsTI-1, TSA, LeIF, LPG-3); PBMCs from HLA-A2+ and HLA-A2- L. major-recovered individuals
extractions.metric: % CD8+/IFN-γ+ T cells, cutoff-based responder rate, ELISpot, ELISA
extractions.baseline: HLA-A2- recovered individuals (control), HLA-A2+ healthy donors (control)
extractions.result: 31.6% and 13.3% of HLA-A2+ recovered persons responded above cutoff for pools II and IV respectively

extractions.problem: No specific antiviral therapeutics exist for MERS-CoV; in silico prediction of siRNAs targeting conserved viral regions could accelerate oligonucleotide-based therapeutic development.
extractions.method: Integrated bioinformatics approach (i-SCORE, RNAxs, RNAfold) for siRNA prediction targeting MERS-CoV orf1ab gene, followed by in vitro validation via reverse transfection in Vero cells and quantitative real-time PCR.
extractions.finding: 21 functional off-target-reduced siRNAs were selected from 462 candidates; siRNA-1 and siRNA-4 showed the best inhibition of viral replication in Vero cells with no cytotoxicity.
extractions.dataset: 17 full-genome MERS-CoV sequences (human and camel isolates, 92.3-99.9% identity); Vero cell line
extractions.metric: Ct values (qRT-PCR), cytotoxicity, free energy folding, seed-duplex stability
extractions.result: siRNA-1 best at 0.1 nM, siRNA-4 best at 5.0 and 10 nM in cell lysate; 21/462 siRNAs passed stringent filtering

extractions.problem: Active anti-leukemia components in Traditional Chinese Medicine herbs like Indigo naturalis and their precise molecular targets remain unidentified.
extractions.method: Comprehensive 2D K562/cell membrane chromatography (CMC) for active component screening, combined with in silico target identification (PharmMapper, TargetHunter), validated by cell viability (CCK-8), apoptosis (Annexin V-FITC), and Western blot.
extractions.finding: Three active components (indirubin, tryptanthrin, isorhamnetin) were identified; Src kinase was identified as isorhamnetin's membrane target (Kd=3.81 μM), mediating anti-leukemia effects through G2/M cell cycle arrest.
extractions.dataset: K562 leukemia cell line; Indigo naturalis (Qingdai) extract; 24 chemical components identified by Q-TOFMS
extractions.metric: Cell viability (CCK-8), apoptosis rate, dissociation constant (Kd), retention time on CMC
extractions.baseline: Imatinib (membrane receptor drug) and dexamethasone (intracellular receptor drug) as CMC controls
extractions.result: Kd(Src-isorhamnetin)=3.81 μM; isorhamnetin induces dose-dependent apoptosis and G2/M arrest in K562 cells

extractions.problem: How mechanical confinement and hypoxia jointly regulate tumor cell aggregate growth dynamics is poorly understood, limiting predictive modeling of early tumor growth.
extractions.method: Dual in vitro-in silico approach: CT26 tumor spheroids grown in alginate capsules (spherical and tubular) with tunable confinement, combined with a continuum mathematical model coupling cell mass fraction, oxygen diffusion, and internal pressure.
extractions.finding: Two distinct confinement regimes were identified: strong confinement (spherical capsules) causes homogeneous pressure rise above inhibitory threshold, while weak confinement (tubes) allows continued tip proliferation with central pressure buildup and hypoxia-induced necrosis.
extractions.dataset: CT26 colon carcinoma cells in alginate spherical capsules (90 μm inner radius) and tubular capsules
extractions.metric: Aggregate radius vs time, internal pressure, oxygen mass fraction thresholds
extractions.result: Growth inhibition pressure threshold 800-2000 Pa; confluence at tc=66h (spherical); oxygen inhibition below 8×10⁻⁶ mass fraction

extractions.problem: Whether noncoding RNAs (miRNAs, lncRNAs) regulate signaling pathways involved in SARS-CoV infection is not fully investigated, limiting identification of novel therapeutic targets.
extractions.method: Bioinformatic network analysis using STRING (PPI), GSEA/KEGG (pathway enrichment), MiRTarBase (miRNA-mRNA interactions), miRWalk2.0 (miRNA-lncRNA interactions), and cytoHubba (hub node identification).
extractions.finding: TGF-beta signaling pathway was identified as the most enriched pathway (11 genes); hub miRNAs (hsa-let-7a, miR-181a, miR-20b, miR-17, miR-27a) and hub lncRNAs (MALAT1, XIST) were identified as potential therapeutic targets.
extractions.dataset: SARS-CoV proteome (15 viral proteins) vs human proteome (40 host proteins); 293 protein-protein interactions
extractions.metric: P-value, gene count per pathway, hub degree (cytoHubba)
extractions.result: TGF-beta pathway P=6.99E-21 (11 genes); 5 hub miRNAs and 2 hub lncRNAs identified; 293 virus-host PPI interactions

extractions.problem: The molecular mechanism of quercetin's inhibition of acetylcholinesterase (AChE) and its neuroprotective potential against Aβ25-35-induced oxidative stress are not fully elucidated.
extractions.method: Multispectroscopic analysis (fluorescence quenching, CD, UV-vis), enzyme kinetics (Ellman's method, Lineweaver-Burk), molecular docking, MD simulation, and PC12 cell neuroprotection assay (MTT, MDA, GSH, ROS).
extractions.finding: Quercetin inhibits AChE in a reversible mixed manner by binding at the peripheral aromatic site and extending into the active center; it synergizes with galantamine and protects PC12 cells from Aβ25-35-induced oxidative stress.
extractions.dataset: AChE from Electrophorus electricus; PC12 cells with Aβ25-35-induced oxidative stress model
extractions.metric: IC50, binding constant (Ka), fluorescence quenching, MDA/GSH/ROS levels
extractions.baseline: Galantamine hydrobromide (IC50=0.29 μM); taxifolin (IC50=54.01 μM); quercitrin (IC50=94.5 μM)
extractions.result: IC50=4.59±0.27 μM; Ka=(5.52±0.05)×10⁴ L/mol at 25°C; synergistic inhibition with galantamine

extractions.problem: Whether BCG vaccine can generate cross-reactive T cells against SARS-CoV-2 through shared epitopes is unknown, despite epidemiological associations between BCG vaccination and reduced COVID-19 severity.
extractions.method: Protein BLAST for similar 9-amino acid sequences between SARS-CoV-2 and M. bovis BCG, followed by HLA class I binding affinity prediction using IEDB and NetMHCpan 4.1 for 15 common HLA alleles.
extractions.finding: Seven similar 9-amino acid sequences (1 aa substitution) were found between SARS-CoV-2 and M. bovis; 6 of 7 peptide pairs showed moderate to high HLA class I binding affinity, supporting possible cross-reactive T cell generation.
extractions.dataset: SARS-CoV-2 proteome (MN908947.3) vs M. bovis BCG str. Tokyo 172; 15 common HLA-A/-B/-C alleles (Japan, USA)
extractions.metric: HLA binding affinity (Percentile Rank <10), number of shared amino acid sequences
extractions.result: 7 similar 9-mer sequences found; 6/7 pairs bind common HLA class I with Percentile Rank <10; >40 identical heptamer sequences
